name: Sol (calibration world)
note: host_star at the galactic origin makes this the Sol world used by the section 3 validation (observer at origin == Earth vantage). The observatory CONSUMES this file; it never generates it.

planet.orbit_note: i_deg/Omega_deg place the orbital-plane normal at the real ecliptic pole (RA 270 deg, Dec +66.5607 deg -- Earth's true ecliptic-equator obliquity), so the north_pole_inertial above (Earth's real celestial pole, correctly at dec 90) sits 23.4 deg away from it -- axial_tilt_deg is then the ACTUAL angle Stage 2 sees, not a decorative field. (i_deg=0 previously put the orbital normal AT dec 90 too, coincident with the pole: zero effective tilt, no seasons -- found while building the Phase 8 dossier's solstice/day-length scan, which needs the real geometry.)
